title: The Future of ‘Star Wars,’ Marvel, Pixar, and Disney’s Movie Monopoly. Plus: Our Summer Movie Awards and Paul Downs Colaizzo on ‘Brittany Runs a Marathon’ | The Big Picture
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris pick through D23's Star Wars, Marvel, and Pixar reveals, hand out the summer movie awards (unanimous winner: 'Once Upon a Time in Hollywood'), then Sean talks to Paul Downs Colaizzo about directing 'Brittany Runs a Marathon.'

segments.heading: D23 and Disney's Movie Monopoly
segments.note: Star Wars, Marvel, Pixar, and the legacy titles, all at once

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Amanda's go-to party trick is reciting the entire plot from memory; she always nails the Luke/Leia/Han tension and always forgets that the Death Star gets destroyed.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: The D23 cast reveal: Angelina Jolie, Salma Hayek, Brian Tyree Henry, Gemma Chan, Kumail Nanjiani, and Kit Harington as Black Knight, joining fellow Game of Thrones alum Richard Madden. Chris doesn't know who any of these characters are yet.

films.title: Onward
films.note: Amanda's outline notes on the new Pixar film, which stars Chris Pratt as one of two elf brothers who lost their father young.

films.title: Soul
films.note: The other new Pixar reveal, with Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey, built around the question of where a soul comes from.

films.title: Inside Out
films.note: Comes up as the group tries to place Tina Fey among Pixar's other tearjerkers (that's actually Amy Poehler).

films.title: Jungle Cruise
films.note: Dwayne Johnson's ride adaptation, directed by Jaume Collet-Serra, whose résumé (The Shallows, The Commuter, Run All Night, Nonstop) suggests a much darker movie than the marketing implies.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: Chris still hasn't seen it, but concedes it's soaking up an outsized share of the pop-culture airwaves.

films.title: Lady and the Tramp
films.note: The live-action remake gets its first look at D23.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: Announced roughly a thousand days out, which strikes Chris as a strange flex; Amanda reads it as Disney simply confirming what everyone already suspected — that it owns the whole board now.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: No footage, no clear sense anyone's asking for it — except, Amanda notes, nobody was asking for it in 2009 either, and James Cameron just made the two biggest movies of all time twice.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Bob Iger name-checked the Fox films Disney inherited when explaining a down earnings quarter; this is the one they're actually backing, and Sean expects it to be a real awards contender this fall.

films.title: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie
films.note: Leaked out of D23 season rather than announced at it. Amanda's default skepticism about victory-lap revivals; Chris trusts it because Better Call Saul already proved this team clears the bar.

films.title: Deadwood: The Movie
films.note: Left Amanda with a bad taste — the same problem, she says, that sank the Veronica Mars movie: compressed, fan-servicey, missing what made the original work.

segments.heading: Best Performance in a Bad Movie

films.title: Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark
films.note: Chris's best-supporting-in-a-bad-movie pick: Austin Abrams, doing more with the role than it requires.

films.title: Always Be My Maybe
films.note: Keanu Reeves's cameo gets a shoutout — briefly the only thing anyone talked about, then instantly forgotten.

films.title: Late Night
films.note: Sean's pick for Emma Thompson; he's also still trying to figure out what happened to Hugh Dancy's character.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: The obvious answer for both Leonardo DiCaprio and, per the DGA podcast conversation between Paul Thomas Anderson and Quentin Tarantino, an increasingly hard-to-ignore Brad Pitt.

films.title: Luce
films.note: Naomi Watts, as good as she's been in a long time — they table the rest of the discussion for the surprise category.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: Florence Pugh's other nod this summer.

films.title: Yesterday
films.note: Himesh Patel, singing Beatles songs live at Wembley for an audience that had mostly never seen him before.

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: Beanie Feldstein, a performance the group feels got swallowed by the will-it-flop discourse around the movie's opening weekend.

films.title: Plus One
films.note: Maya Erskine, who Amanda calls transcendent even though the movie is structured around the guy character.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: Honor Swinton Byrne, cast two weeks before filming with essentially no preparation — which is exactly why the performance works.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: Jamie Bell, in a movie Sean says has already been forgotten the way Bohemian Rhapsody never was.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Diana Lin and Zhao Shuzhen as the mother and grandmother.

films.title: The Nightingale
films.note: Baykali Ganambarr, incredible in what Amanda calls a genuinely brutal watch.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Sean picks the out-of-time montage of Cliff driving Rick home through a changing L.A.; Amanda picks Brad Pitt on the roof.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: Chris's pick: the cold open, which he calls as punishing as filmmaking gets.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Sean saw it on the Disney lot with a crowd that lost its mind when the hammer found Captain America's hand.

films.title: Plus One
films.note: Amanda rewound and rewatched Maya Erskine's wedding dance, something she says she never does.

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: Charlize Theron's hostage-negotiation scene.

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: Killed at SXSW, then made about $14 million — less than Booksmart. The group's theory: a political rom-com released a week after Endgame, starring two people over 35, in a genre nobody goes to theaters for anymore.

segments.heading: Worst Reboot
segments.note: the category that swallowed remakes, relaunches, and cinematic universes

films.title: Men in Black: International
films.note: Sean's pick, one of the year's bigger what-the-fucks.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Debated as a reboot since it launches a new corner of the Fast universe.

films.title: The Hustle
films.note: A remake of Dirty Rotten Scoundrels that Chris argues is a better film than Hobbs & Shaw, box office aside.

films.title: Shaft
films.note: Chris only caught it because Netflix owned the rights in Europe, where he was traveling.

films.title: Dark Phoenix
films.note: Not technically a reboot, but gets a special shoutout as maybe the worst movie of the summer, and the one that gave Chris and Sean a false fire alarm 15 seconds from the end.

films.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
films.note: Sean's come around on it, mostly thanks to a weak summer making everything look better by comparison.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: Sean's other nominee; Amanda refuses to engage with it as a category entry.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The consensus answer, arrived at without much debate.

films.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 — Parabellum
films.note: Amanda's pick, though she thinks it runs long.

films.title: The Secret Life of Pets 2
films.note: Chris, only half-joking, citing the commercials as proof the barrel is being scraped.

films.title: Luce
films.note: The most divisive pick of the episode: Chris found it genuinely surprising and interesting; Amanda thought it raised hot-button issues it never developed and that the lead performance didn't land.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: The unanimous pick.

films.title: Head Count
films.note: Chris's recommendation for anyone hunting for a lesser-known scare on iTunes.

films.title: The Dead Don't Die
films.note: Not a traditional horror movie, but worth it if you're into Jim Jarmusch's tone, per Chris.

segments.heading: What the F*** Movie of the Season

films.title: Godzilla: King of the Monsters
films.note: A cast stacked with Millie Bobby Brown, Charles Dance, Kyle Chandler, and Vera Farmiga, and nobody in the room saw it, talked about it, or knows anyone who did — despite the prior two films in the MonsterVerse doing well.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: Amanda's pick; she went in on a tip and came out having discovered a filmmaker.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Sean's alternate pick.

films.title: Sword of Trust
films.note: Chris's pick: a Lynn Shelton movie with Marc Maron, Jillian Bell, and Michaela Watkins that plays like a small '70s character piece.

films.title: The Art of Self-Defense
films.note: Chris's other pick, Riley Stearns's hand-to-hand-combat psychodrama with Jesse Eisenberg, watched in the kitchen of a South by Southwest Airbnb.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Unanimous. Sean saw it twice in theaters; the group agrees it's the only movie from the summer they're still thinking about, and that it beats out even Fleabag, Succession, Chernobyl, and Mindhunter as the best thing they watched all season.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.note: the writer-director on his first film, which premiered at Sundance and sold to Amazon

films.title: Brittany Runs a Marathon
films.note: Colaizzo started writing it in 2011 about his real-life best friend and roommate, then spent two and a half years developing the script with Tobey Maguire and Matt Plouffe's company before convincing them to let him direct it himself. Jillian Bell lost the same 40 pounds her character does over the shoot, timed against a custom prosthetic that had to be remade mid-production when her weight loss outpaced the fitting.

films.title: The Big Sick
films.note: The reason Colaizzo wanted Amazon as a distributor — he thought it handled a small, true-story indie with both theatrical reach and home accessibility.

films.title: Carnal Knowledge
films.note: His answer to the show's closing question, the last great thing he'd seen — a Mike Nichols film he watched jet-lagged two nights before the interview.

El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie.title: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie

Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark.title: Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
